rulings.Case Title: LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC VS. MARTIN LEE ENG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Consolidate Cases Filed By Plaintiff Legal Recovery, Llc Against Martin Eng
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 18, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANTS LOMBARD FLATS, LLC, KEARNY WASHINGTON LLC, JOANNE ENG'S Motion To Consolidate Cases Filed By Plaintiff Legal Recovery, Llc Against Martin Eng The hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am. Defendants Lombard Flats, LLC, Kearny Washington, LLC and Joanne Eng's motion to consolidate this case with case 542378 is denied. While the two cases relate to the same general subject matter, they are in very different procedural postures and consolidating the cases will disserve judicial economy. This case is less than a month from trial, while a judgment has been entered in case 542378. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JERMAINE HORTON VS. FCA US LLC.,A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 18, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANTS FCA US LLC, SAN LEANDRO CAR STOP, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendants FCA US LLC and San Leandro Car Stop, LLC's motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff Jermaine Horton's fourth cause of action for fraudulent concealment and prayer for punitive damages is GRANTED. A defendant shifts the burden of production to plaintiff if it can "present evidence that the plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence." (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 854-55.) Defendants showed via plaintiff's discovery responses his lack of evidence that FCA US knew of the defect in the Totally Integrated Power Module (TIPM) before plaintiff purchased the subject vehicle. Thus, defendants shifted the evidentiary burden. Plaintiff's assertions of prior knowledge and fraudulent concealment are not supported by his discovery responses or documentary evidence. The Warranty Claim Report and Customer Assistance Inquiry Record illustrate problems plaintiff allegedly experienced with his vehicle after purchase, but do not indicate prior knowledge of a TIPM defect in the vehicle. The Recall Index (Semaan Dec. Ex. 2) merely shows that two recalls applicable to plaintiff's type of vehicle occurred prior to his purchase; it does not support the notion that FCA US had prior knowledge of TIPM defects in the vehicle or that it concealed such defects. Because no clear and convincing evidence of fraud exists, punitive damages are unattainable and the prayer for them also fails. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JANUARY 18, 2019, LINE 1 PLAINTIFF KINSON WONG'S Motion To Reopen Discovery Based Upon New Trial Date Pro Tem Judge Aaron Minnis, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. rulings.Case Title: MARIA ISABEL DELGADO VS. PRIMERICA LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, Motion To Quash Or Strike Defendants Subpoena For Plaintiffs Medical Records, And For Sanctions, Against Defendants Primerica Life Insurance Company And Primerica Financial Services Insurance Marketing, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JANUARY 18, 2019, LINE 2 PLAINTIFF MARIA DELGADO'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, Motion To Quash Or Strike Defendants Subpoena For Plaintiffs Medical Records, And For Sanctions, Against Defendants Primerica Life Insurance Company And Primerica Financial Services Insurance Marketing, Inc. Pro Tem Judge Aaron Minnis, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted as to Requests for Production (RFP) Nos. 25, 26; Special Interrogatories Nos. 1, 2, 5, 15, 16, except that Nos. 15-16 shall be limited to the court and case number of any lawsuits filed in California within the last 10 years; and Form Interrogatories Nos. 12.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.6. Primerica shall serve Code-compliant responses and documents within 30 days, along with a sufficiently detailed privilege log for any information withheld or redacted on privilege or other grounds. Denied as to RFP Nos. 29, 30, 31, and Special Interrogatory No. 16. Plaintiff failed to show these overbroad requests are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. The motion to quash subpoenas for plaintiff's medical records is Denied. Plaintiff delayed filing the motion to quash until after the records had already been produced. However, plaintiff is not precluded from challenging the admissibility or relevance of plaintiff's medical records at trial, and plaintiff's medical records shall be treated as confidential for 60 days to allow the parties to enter into a stipulated protective order to address plaintiff's privacy concerns. Sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to aaron@minnisandsmallets.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = AM

rulings.Case Title: CROWN BUILDING MAINTENANCE, INC. VS. METRO SERVICES GROUP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or, Alternatively, Stay The Jams Arbitration Against Defendant Derek Schulze Pending The Outcome Of This Action Pursuant To Ccp Section 1281.2(C)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 18, 2019, Line 12, DEFENDANTS JEFF DACHENHAUS, MARK NOLAN, DEREK SCHULZE'S Motion To Dismiss Or, Alternatively, Stay The Jams Arbitration Against Defendant Derek Schulze Pending The Outcome Of This Action Pursuant To Ccp Section 1281.2(C) Defendant Derrek Schulze's motion to dismiss the arbitration is DENIED, as the court lacks jurisdiction to grant such relief. (Titan/Value Equities Group, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1994) 29 Cal.App.4th 482, 489.) Schulze's motion to stay the arbitration under CCP §1281.2(c) pending the outcome of this court case is GRANTED. The at-issue employment agreement states that California law applies. (Id. at §14.1.) Section 14.2 of that agreement merely provides that, in an arbitration, California substantive law applies; it does not bar application of CCP §1281.2(c) to our court action. The third-party litigation exception of §1281.2(c) applies when "(1) '[a] party to the arbitration agreement is also a party to a pending court action¿with a third party'; (2) the third-party action 'aris[es] out of the same transaction or series of related transactions' and (3) there is a possibility of conflicting rulings on a common issue of law or fact." (Acquire II, Ltd. V. Colton Real Estate Grp. (2013) 213 Cal.App.4th 959, 967-68.) Here, court defendants Jeff Dachenhaus and Mark Nolan are the third parties and plaintiff Crown Building Maintenance, Inc. is a party to both the court action and the arbitration. As the court action and arbitration cover much the same ground, there would be a possibility of conflicting rulings. (See CCP §1281.2(c).) Moreover, the court case is further advanced than the arbitration. There has been extensive discovery in the former, with an August 2019 trial date. In contrast, the arbitration's initial scheduling conference occurred only last month. So efficiency would be served by a stay of the arbitration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Schulze is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ALIAKSEI HAEDZEYCHIK VS. MEL BRACKEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay The Proceedings Pending The Outcome Of Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 18, 2019, Line 13, DEFENDANTS MEL BRACKEN, READISUITE LLC'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay The Proceedings Pending The Outcome Of Arbitration. Defendants Mel Bracken and ReadiSuite LLC's motion to compel arbitration and to stay litigation pending arbitration is GRANTED. Per the Dispute Resolution Protocol (DRP) in the TriNet Terms and Conditions Agreement (TCA), the Federal Arbitration Act applies to any dispute arising out of plaintiff's employment with ReadiSuite. DRP §9(a) expressly identifies ReadiSuite as a third-party beneficiary that may enforce the arbitration provision. Defendants did not waive arbitration; they pled an arbitration agreement as their first affirmative defense. Further, defendants sought to stay discovery pending this motion, and plaintiff has not been materially prejudiced by the slight delay. Whether the DRP is a contract of adhesion is disputed, but the burden is on the party opposing arbitration. (Green Tree Financial Corp.-Alabama v. Randolph (2000) 531 U.S. 79, 91-92.) Plaintiff does not carry that burden; his declaration conflicts with TriNet's regarding an option to reject the TCA and DRP terms. The screenshot showing an "Accept" button is irrelevant as it does not pertain to the TCA or DRP. An arbitration agreement is not unenforceable because it is an employment condition. (McManus v. CIBC World Markets Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 92, 92-103.) The TCA is only five pages long; the DRP was marked with a bolded header; a link was presented to JAMS Rules; and the TCA was provided to plaintiff as an email attachment for review and he never objected to its terms. The PAGA waiver's alleged substantive unconscionability is irrelevant as plaintiff makes no PAGA claim. Likewise, the unilateral modification term is not at issue. The confidentiality term is not unconscionable as it allows for disclosures if required by law. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: BRANDI PIXTON VS. TORIAN HOLDINGS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay The Litigation Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 18, 2019, Line 14, DEFENDANTS TORIAN HOLDINGS, CONCORD AUTOMOBILE DEALERSHIP, LLC'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay The Litigation Proceedings. OFF CALENDAR. The court will sign the parties' proposed stipulation and order that grants arbitration and stays this action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: NIMER MASSIS ET AL VS. RECE RIVER CORP. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CSM-18-858880 WITH CASE CGC-18-571523
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 18, 2019, Line 15, PLAINTIFFS NIMER MASSIS, JENNIFER NUSHWAT'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CSM-18-858880 WITH CASE CGC-18-571523. The motion to consolidate is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Case CGC-18-571523 is consolidated with CSM-18-858880 for all purposes. The court designates CGC-18-571523 as the lead action and all future filings shall be made in that case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Massis is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
